---
title: "Course Content"
---

Each class session has lecture materials, readings, and videos covering the week's topics. Work through these materials before attempting the assignments and lab exercises.

## Quick Access

| Week | Content Page | Lectures |
|------|--------------|--------|
| Week 1-2 | [HTML & Introduction](/content/week-01.qmd) | [{{< fa desktop >}}](/lectures/index.qmd) |
| Week 2-3 | [HTML Elements & CSS](/content/week-02.qmd) | [{{< fa desktop >}}](/lectures/index.qmd) |
| Week 3-5 | [CSS Styling & JavaScript](/content/week-03.qmd) | [{{< fa desktop >}}](/lectures/index.qmd) |
| Week 4 | [JavaScript Functions & Events](/content/week-04.qmd) | [{{< fa desktop >}}](/lectures/index.qmd) |
| Week 5 | [DOM & ES6+](/content/week-05.qmd) | [{{< fa desktop >}}](/lectures/index.qmd) |
| Week 6-7 | [Revision & Mid-Sem](/content/week-06.qmd) | [{{< fa desktop >}}](/lectures/index.qmd) |
| Week 8 | [Servlets & JSP Intro](/content/week-08.qmd) | [{{< fa desktop >}}](/lectures/index.qmd) |
| Week 9 | [JSP Advanced & MVC](/content/week-09.qmd) | [{{< fa desktop >}}](/lectures/index.qmd) |
| Week 10 | [Spring Framework](/content/week-10.qmd) | [{{< fa desktop >}}](/lectures/index.qmd) |
| Week 11 | [Spring Boot & Data](/content/week-11.qmd) | [{{< fa desktop >}}](/lectures/index.qmd) |
| Week 12 | [REST & Final Project](/content/week-12.qmd) | [{{< fa desktop >}}](/lectures/index.qmd) |

## How to Use This Section

For each week:

1. **Read the assigned materials** listed on each content page
2. **Watch the video lectures** (if available) to understand key concepts
3. **Review the slides and examples** to see how concepts are applied
4. **Complete the lab exercises** to get hands-on practice
5. **Start the assignments early** to have time for questions
6. **Take the quiz for the topic** in [Interactive Quizzes](/quiz/index.qmd) to check understanding

## Course Content by Week

### Module 1: Web Fundamentals (Weeks 1-2)

- [**Week 1-2**: HTML & Introduction](/content/week-01.qmd)
  - Introduction to Web Designing
  - History of Web & Internet
  - HTML Basics & Structure
  - HTML Grouping, Lists, Images, Tables, Forms
  - Introduction to CSS

### Module 2: CSS & Styling (Weeks 2-3)

- [**Week 2-3**: HTML Elements & CSS](/content/week-02.qmd)
  - HTML Lists & Images
  - HTML Hyperlinks & Tables
  - HTML Forms & Form Elements
  - Introduction to CSS
  - CSS Units, Selectors & Styling

- [**Week 3-5**: CSS Styling & JavaScript](/content/week-03.qmd)
  - CSS Box Model & Positioning
  - Bootstrap Framework Basics
  - JavaScript Introduction & Variables
  - Data Types, Operators & Control Flow
  - Functions & Callbacks

### Module 3: JavaScript & DOM (Weeks 4-5)

- [**Week 4**: JavaScript Functions & Events](/content/week-04.qmd)
  - JavaScript Functions & Scope
  - Control Flow & Loops
  - Events & Event Handling
  - Event Listeners & Callbacks

- [**Week 5**: DOM & ES6+](/content/week-05.qmd)
  - Document Object Model (DOM)
  - Selecting & Modifying Elements
  - Form Validation with JavaScript
  - ES6+ Features & Modern JavaScript

### Module 4: Server-Side Development (Weeks 8-9)

- [**Week 8-9**: Servlets, JSP & MVC](/content/week-08.qmd)
  - Servlet Architecture & Life Cycle
  - HTTP GET & POST Requests
  - Session Tracking & Cookies
  - Java Server Pages (JSP) Basics
  - JSP Implicit Objects & Actions

### Module 5: Spring Framework (Weeks 10-12)

- [**Week 10**: Spring Framework Fundamentals](/content/week-10.qmd)
  - Spring Core & Dependency Injection
  - Inversion of Control (IoC) Container
  - Bean Scopes & Auto-wiring
  - Aspect-Oriented Programming (AOP)

- [**Week 11**: Spring Boot & Spring Data](/content/week-11.qmd)
  - Spring Boot Configuration
  - Spring Data JPA
  - Spring Security Basics
  - Building REST APIs

- [**Week 12**: REST APIs & Deployment](/content/week-12.qmd)
  - RESTful Web Services
  - REST Best Practices
  - Testing & Deployment
  - Final Project

## Tips for Success

::: {.callout-tip}
### Study Tips

- **Don't just read—practice!** Type out the code examples yourself
- **Experiment with variations** of the examples to deepen your understanding
- **Use browser developer tools** to inspect and debug your code
- **Build something real** - apply what you learn to create actual web pages
- **Ask questions** if you're stuck—web development has a steep learning curve!
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
### Common Pitfalls to Avoid

- Skipping the fundamentals (HTML/CSS) to jump to JavaScript
- Not testing your code in multiple browsers
- Forgetting to close tags or use proper syntax
- Copying code without understanding what it does
- Not using version control (Git) for your projects
:::

## Additional Resources

For supplementary materials, tutorials, and reference documentation, visit the [Resources](/resource/index.qmd) section.
